lifecycle.name: ENTRY
lifecycle.summary: Routes incoming work to the correct lifecycle. Creates tickets if needed. Determines the base branch and creates initial branches. Every change must be tied to a GitHub issue.

phase.name: IDENTIFY_ENTRY

actions.action: Determine which entry point applies based on the human's request: - EXISTING_TICKET: A GitHub issue already exists (the human says "work on #42" or the orchestrator picks one from "gh issue list --state open"). - SPEC_FILE: The human points to a spec file or provides a large feature description that needs to be broken into multiple tickets before work can begin. - AD_HOC_REQUEST: The human gives an instruction directly (e.g. "add a logout button"). This is NOT a ticket yet.
If the request does not match any recognized entry point or appears out of scope, apply scope-enforcement: push back respectfully and offer to create a new ticket instead.

phase.on-failure: Stop and ask the human to clarify their request

phase.name: TICKET_AND_BRANCH_SETUP

actions.action: Run "gh issue view {number}" to read the issue and confirm it is understood.
Read the spec file in full.
Create an epic issue: gh issue create --title "..." --body "..." --label epic
Decompose the spec into individual feature/task issues. For dependency tracking, add a task list in the epic body listing child issues.

Create a GitHub issue: gh issue create --title "..." --body "..." --label enhancement|bug|task Never implement an ad-hoc request without a ticket. If the human insists on skipping, explain that every change must be tracked and push back respectfully.

Determine the base branch. Check the current git context first — if already on a feature branch (e.g. mid-epic work), default to that branch as the base. Otherwise, reason about the appropriate base: - If the context makes the base branch obvious, use it. - If ambiguous, ask the human which base branch to use (main or an existing feature branch). The base branch is where the ticket's branch will be created from and where its PR will target.

phase.on-failure: Stop and report the failure to the human before continuing

phase.name: SPEC_FILE_APPROVAL

actions.action: Present the full ticket breakdown to the human for approval (see human-gate behavior). Include: epic issue number, all child issue numbers and titles, dependency ordering, and selected base branch (main or feature branch).
Wait for explicit human approval before proceeding.

phase.on-failure: Stop and report the failure to the human
phase.on-rejection: Return to TICKET_AND_BRANCH_SETUP to revise the ticket breakdown based on human feedback, then re-present

phase.name: BRANCH_SETUP
phase.actor: Orchestrator launches a branch setup agent (light tier)

actions.action: If a feature branch was selected as the base branch: Create the feature branch from main: git checkout -b EPIC/{number}-{desc}
For multi-ticket work (SPEC_FILE or epic): pick the first ready child issue (gh issue list --state open). This child issue number becomes the active ticket for the PLANNING and IMPLEMENTATION phases.
Push the branch to remote: git push -u origin {branch-name}

phase.on-failure: Stop and report the failure to the human
